dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8369 IH: Keep Immigration Enforcement Out of Schools Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Cherfilus-McCormick introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To prohibit Federal education funds from being made available to any educational agency or institution that releases any education records or other student information for purposes of immigration enforcement.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Keep Immigration Enforcement Out of Schools Act or the No ICE in Schools Act.

section.header: Prohibition of release of education records or other student information for immigration enforcement purposes
section.text: Section 444 of the General Education Provisions Act (20 U.S.C. 1232g) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, no funds shall be made available under any applicable program to any educational agency or institution which, for purposes of immigration enforcement, has a policy or practice of releasing, or providing access to—

paragraph.text: any other information with respect to a student, including personally identifiable information or directory information,

subsection.continuation-text: unless there is written consent from the student’s parents specifying the records or information to be released, the reasons for such release, and to whom, and with a copy of the records or information to be released to the student’s parents and the student if desired by the parents.
